rationale: The citing paper appears to reference Novemsky et al. (2007) only once, in a parenthetical "e.g." citation to note that using only three-option sets is consistent with prior compromise-choice research. The paper does not develop or test the preference-fluency account from Novemsky et al., and it does not use that work as a core basis for its hypotheses or mechanism. Functionally, the citation serves as a background methodological precedent rather than a distinct building block of the article's contribution.
